project: Author GEO Website — Tier 1 (static, Cloudflare Pages) starter
purpose: Describe-don't-store artifact. Enumerates every file the Tier-1 Astro starter needs. Schema-critical files carry FULL contents; routine page templates are described with stub_for_worker=true for a coding agent to expand. Materialize the whole repo in one pass once a real toolchain (build host) exists.

target_stack.version_policy: DECISION (Malorie 2026-07-20): build against the LATEST current Astro (v7.x, verified live in the build env as 7.1.3) and PIN TO THE MAJOR in package.json ("astro": "^7") so patch/minor updates flow but a breaking major (v8) never lands unbidden. Rationale: a teaching product needs a stable, documented target, not a moving one; ^7 gives students today's stable behavior while keeping non-breaking fixes automatic. When scaffolding, capture the exact resolved version (e.g. via committed package-lock.json) for reproducibility.
target_stack.tier2_host: Cloudflare Workers + D1 (output: server, @astrojs/cloudflare) — SAME codebase, added later
target_stack.note_cloudflare_env: CONFIRMED CURRENT for v7 (Architect verified against docs.astro.build 2026-07-20): Cloudflare env access is `import { env } from 'cloudflare:workers'` — the current @astrojs/cloudflare adapter EXPLICITLY documents this as the pattern that REPLACED the removed `Astro.locals.runtime.env`. Our sample is on the correct side of that migration. PIN REQUIREMENT: pin @astrojs/cloudflare to a version new enough to carry the `cloudflare:workers` env import (the adapter major that removed Astro.locals.runtime); verify the resolved adapter version at scaffold time with `astro build`.
target_stack.note_content_collections: CONFIRMED CURRENT for v7 (Architect verified against docs.astro.build 2026-07-20): content.config.ts API is unchanged from our v6 design — defineCollection from 'astro:content', glob/file loaders from 'astro/loaders', z from 'astro/zod', reference() for cross-collection links (Author/Book/Series). Stable since the v5 content-layer landing. No code change needed.
target_stack.vendor_note: Cloudflare acquired the Astro team — first-party pairing.

product_decisions_locked.lead_optin_mode: config option (single/double toggle in author config)
product_decisions_locked.comps_placement: inline on book/series/hub pages ONLY — no standalone Comp routes
product_decisions_locked.repo_model: git-clonable TEMPLATE REPO, engine/content-separated, setup skill shipped inside it. Generator deferred (the author's AI is the customizer). Authors' repos descend from the template as git upstream so schema/JSON-LD improvements ship via `git pull upstream main` — the only model that solves the update story.

engine_content_split.principle: Separate ENGINE (Malorie owns + updates) from CONTENT (author owns + edits) so upstream pulls almost never conflict. This is what makes `git pull upstream main` a boring, safe update path for non-developers.
engine_content_split.update_flow: Author's AI runs `git pull upstream main`; engine files update, content files untouched -> clean merge. Setup skill documents this. Escape hatch: an `update` verb in the local skill wrapping the pull if merges intimidate.

content_model_entities: Comp (required descriptive hook, sameAs disambiguation; inline only)

files.purpose: Declares Astro + adapter deps and scripts.
files.notes: PIN astro to the major: "astro": "^7" (build against current v7.x, verified 7.1.3). @astrojs/cloudflare (for Tier 2), zod (bundled with astro:content). scripts: dev/build/preview. COMMIT package-lock.json for a reproducible resolved version.

files.purpose: Astro config. Tier 1 = static; a commented Tier-2 block flips to server + cloudflare adapter.
files.notes: output:'static' for Tier 1. Include site: URL for canonical + sitemap. Comment block showing the Tier-2 switch (output:'server', adapter: cloudflare()).

files.purpose: Cloudflare config; D1 binding for Tier 2 (commented in Tier 1).
THE opinionated 6-entity Zod schema. The pedagogical enforcement layer — an author literally cannot ship a book without a cover, buy link, and description.
The interface that makes Tier 2 a SUPERSET of Tier 1. FileSource (Astro content collections) today; D1Source (database) later — renderer/JSON-LD never change.
Tier-1 ContentSource backed by astro:content collections.
files.notes: Implements ContentSource by calling getCollection('books') etc. and mapping entries to the plain interfaces. Derives series membership from books' series/seriesPosition.

files.purpose: The @graph builder — single canonical #author node referenced everywhere. The GEO payload, derived from ContentSource so it can never drift from visible content.
Pluggable lead adapter #1. Selected by author config.
Pluggable lead adapter #2. Selected by author config.
Lead adapter contract.
files.contents:
export interface Lead { email: string; name?: string; groups?: string[]; }
export interface LeadAdapter { name: string; subscribe(lead: Lead): Promise<{ ok: true }>; }

files.purpose: Cloudflare Pages Function for lead capture. Holds the ESP key server-side; config-driven single/double opt-in.
Author-facing single config surface. Provider choice + opt-in mode + lead groups + site URL.
Base layout: <head>, sitewide WebSite/Organization JSON-LD, breadcrumbs slot.
files.notes: Injects sitewide JSON-LD (WebSite, Organization/Person, BreadcrumbList) via a <script type='application/ld+json'>. Per-page graphs are injected by page templates.

files.purpose: Tiny component that renders a graph object into a ld+json script tag.
Renders comps INLINE (book/series/hub). Visible 'if you like…' text + contributes to page JSON-LD. No standalone route.
files.notes: Per Malorie's decision: comps are inline only, never their own page.

files.purpose: Lead-capture form posting to /api/subscribe. Honeypot + optional Turnstile.
Home / author landing. Author (Person) JSON-LD + featured books.
Book detail. bookNode graph, editions/buy links, inline comps.
Series detail. seriesNode with hasPart, member list, inline comps.
Hub page. hubGraph (CollectionPage + ItemList of DefinedTerm), inline comps. Mirrors aeon14.com/themes.
files.notes: Route name 'themes' matches Malorie's live Aeon 14 convention; could be /hubs — confirm.

files.purpose: Events listing with Event JSON-LD.
Author onboarding: clone, edit config, add content, deploy to Pages, set ESP secrets.
Documents required secrets (MAILERLITE_API_KEY or EMAILOCTOPUS_API_KEY + LIST_ID).

worker_instructions: Files with stub_for_worker=false are ready to write verbatim. Files with stub_for_worker=true are described; expand them consistent with the schema in content.config.ts, the ContentSource interface, and the jsonld.ts builders. Do NOT invent new entities or fields — the 6-entity model is fixed. Keep comps INLINE only. Keep opt-in mode config-driven. Verify with `astro build` on the build host before opening a PR.
